intro:
Converting your hourly rate to an annual salary is essential when comparing job offers, negotiating pay, or planning your finances. A job offering $28/hour sounds very different from one offering $55,000 per year — but are they? Our hourly to salary calculator instantly shows you the annual, monthly, weekly, and daily equivalent of any hourly rate.

The standard full-time calculation is 40 hours per week × 52 weeks = 2,080 hours per year. At $28/hour, that equals $58,240 per year, $4,853.33 per month, or $1,120 per week. Knowing all these figures helps you budget accurately and compare compensation packages.

Remember that salaried employees often receive benefits not counted in hourly rates (health insurance, retirement contributions, paid time off), which can add 20–30% to the total compensation value. Always compare total packages, not just the headline number.
howItWorks: Enter your hourly pay rate, the number of hours you work per week (default 40), and the number of working weeks per year (default 52). The calculator multiplies these to get annual salary, then divides by 12 for monthly, by weeks for weekly, and by weekly hours divided by 5 for daily rate.
interpretationGuide: Annual salary is your gross pay before taxes and deductions. Monthly rate divides by 12. Weekly rate is your hourly rate times hours per week. Daily rate assumes 5 working days per week. All figures are pre-tax — your take-home pay will be lower based on your tax bracket and deductions.

faqs.question: How many hours do salaried employees typically work per year?
faqs.answer: The standard is 2,080 hours (40 hours/week × 52 weeks). However, full-time exempt salaried employees often work more. US workers average around 1,800 hours annually once vacation, holidays, and sick time are accounted for.

faqs.question: Does $28/hour equal a $58,240 annual salary?
faqs.answer: Yes, at the standard 40 hrs/week × 52 weeks = 2,080 hours: $28 × 2,080 = $58,240 gross annual salary. Adjust the hours per week or weeks per year in the calculator if your situation differs.

faqs.question: How do I account for overtime in my annual salary calculation?
faqs.answer: Standard overtime pay in the US is 1.5× your regular rate for hours over 40/week. If you regularly work overtime, calculate: (40 hrs × regular rate + overtime hrs × 1.5× rate) × 52 weeks for a more accurate annual figure.

faqs.question: Should I include benefits when comparing hourly vs. salary?
faqs.answer: Absolutely. Employer-sponsored health insurance can be worth $5,000–$20,000 per year, 401(k) matching 3–6% of salary, and paid vacation 2–4 weeks of pay. A salaried position at $60,000 with strong benefits may be worth more than an hourly position paying $62,000 without benefits.

faqs.question: What is the difference between gross and net pay?
faqs.answer: Gross pay is your total earnings before deductions. Net pay (take-home) is after federal and state income taxes, Social Security (6.2%), Medicare (1.45%), and any voluntary deductions like 401(k) contributions. Effective total deductions are typically 20–35% for most middle-income earners.

faqs.question: How many working weeks are in a year accounting for holidays?
faqs.answer: The US has 11 federal holidays. With 2 weeks vacation (standard entry-level), that is roughly 3 weeks off, leaving 49 working weeks. Many companies provide 15–20 days paid time off. Adjust the 'weeks per year' input to 49 or fewer to reflect actual working time.

faqs.question: Is hourly pay or salary better?
faqs.answer: Hourly pay provides overtime eligibility (time-and-a-half above 40 hours/week for non-exempt workers). Salary offers income predictability, often includes more benefits, and is associated with exempt/professional status. Hourly can pay more in high-overtime roles; salary is better for consistent, flexible hours.

faqs.question: How does part-time hourly pay convert to annual?
faqs.answer: For part-time work at 20 hours/week: $20/hr × 20 hrs × 52 weeks = $20,800/year. Adjust the hours per week in the calculator to match your actual schedule for an accurate annual figure.

faqs.question: What hourly rate equals a $75,000 salary?
faqs.answer: At standard full-time hours: $75,000 ÷ 2,080 hours = $36.06/hour. To find the hourly equivalent of any salary, divide annual salary by your total annual hours worked.

faqs.question: How do I convert a daily rate to annual salary?
faqs.answer: Multiply the daily rate by working days per year. With 260 working days (52 weeks × 5 days), a $300/day rate = $78,000/year. Subtract holidays and vacation days for a more precise figure.
